title:
Commentary on Membership
(Members data presented are as of December 31, 2026)
content:
CAMPUS reported 44 current members as of December 31, 2026. Membership levels remained stable during the period, with no change year-over-year but increasing 2% on a quarter-over-quarter basis. Potential membership totaled 156, remaining unchanged and providing a solid base for future membership development initiatives.

Operational efficiency metrics remain consistent. Members per full-time employee have shown fluctuation over recent quarters, indicating that staffing levels continue to align with current service demand and operational requirements while maintaining balance between member service delivery and operating efficiency.

Shares per member continued to rise during the period, reflecting improved deposit balances relative to membership count. While this trend reflects positive funding behavior among members, it underscores the importance of strengthening member relationships and supporting ongoing deposit retention efforts.

Overall, membership trends remain stable despite modest quarterly fluctuations. The institution retains meaningful opportunity to expand within its existing potential membership base. Continued focus on member engagement, product penetration, and service accessibility will remain important to supporting sustainable membership growth and long-term balance-sheet development.
